current_payload:




Claude responded: Good progress.
Flagged timing concerns and validated component specifications
Flagged timing concerns and validated component specifications
Good progress. Two things I want to flag before we build on top of this — small, but worth catching now rather than after they've propagated into 20 more components:
Button transition at 300ms — that's the slow token, meant more for page-level transitions. Hover/active feedback on a button usually wants fast (150ms) or normal (200ms) so it feels responsive rather than sluggish. Worth double-checking that wasn't an accidental default — if it was a deliberate choice for a specific reason, fine, but I'd lean toward 150ms for hover and maybe 200ms for active/pressed.
"Frosted glass" overlay on out-of-stock — I want to make sure this means a translucent Ink-tinted scrim (e.g. rgba(21,22,26,0.6) over the image) rather than an actual backdrop-filter: blur() effect. Blur-glassmorphism isn't in the anti-patterns list explicitly, but it's a strong aesthetic statement that doesn't come from our token system (no blur token exists), and it'd introduce a texture that nothing else in the app uses. If it's just opacity+color, we're fully on-spec — just flagging so it doesn't sneak in as an unspoken 4th "material."
Everything else — sharp radius discipline, mono for price, 4:5 lock, Signal-only focus rings — sounds exactly right and on-system. Nice work.
Next Step
Let's build the Product Listing Page (PLP) — the grid that consumes the ProductCard component. A few decisions to make together before you build:
Grid rhythm: I'd suggest a 4-column grid on desktop, 2-column tablet, 1-column mobile, with gutter at 32px (from the spacing scale) — gives the cards room to breathe without feeling sparse.
Filter/sort UI: sidebar filters (left-aligned, per the anti-pattern warning against "everything centered") or a horizontal filter bar above the grid? For a premium editorial feel I'd lean toward a minimal horizontal bar (category + sort dropdown only) rather than a heavy faceted sidebar — keeps the page feeling curated rather than like a big-box retailer. Your call though.
Header/nav: does the PLP need the full site header (logo, nav, cart icon) built now, or are we still treating this as an isolated page build with nav coming in a later step?
Let me know your call on filter placement and header scope, and go ahead and build the PLP grid.

